_meta.description: Roadmap derived from the much more mature vermilion project. Executing these tasks would move smgp-disassembly from an early monolithic matching disassembly to a vermilion-like state with broad label coverage, documented subsystems, split modules, decoded data formats, and modding-oriented tooling.
_meta.label_progress: ALL loc_ label definitions eliminated as of batch 41+. 0 loc_ definitions remain (3064 loc_XXXX preservation comments remain as cross-reference anchors). ~1369 new labels added in this final batch covering: HUD asset/tilemap lists, Z80 audio engine internals, sprite frame data cluster, standings/standings sort, per-track data records (all 19 tracks), championship/endgame/podium assets, result screen data, save/load routines, AI placement data, driver standings display assets, audio sequencer, and more.
current_state: Single monolithic smgp.asm source file (~46.7K normalized lines after blank-line cleanup)
Bit-perfect Windows verification exists via verify.bat
Major subsystems now documented: startup, race loop, driving model, rendering/HUD, race flow, Track_data, slope data, and sign parsing
Macro coverage now includes verified VDP helpers plus several exact whole-block helper macros
Open reverse-engineering gaps remain in notes.txt: artwork identification, Z80 audio, music IDs, AI/placement internals, and many remaining internal loc_ branch labels

acceptance: No dc.b/dc.w/dc.l table changes unless output remains byte-identical
New labels follow repo conventions and preserve original ROM address comments when renaming loc_ labels
New RAM, I/O, and struct constants are centralized in constants.asm until the project is ready to split files
editing_rules: Prefer symbolic constants over raw RAM and I/O addresses
Prefer macros only when the expansion is verified byte-identical under ASM68K
Keep solved reverse-engineering notes in source comments, not only in notes.txt
Use Python 3 standard library for automation; notebooks may assist exploration but should not be the only source of truth

always: Run cmd //c verify.bat after every code or data change
If verify fails, isolate the exact cause rather than reverting unrelated work
Preserve ROM layout and branch ranges when renaming labels or introducing macros
before_refactors: Add explanatory comments for routine inputs, outputs, and invariants before moving or splitting code
Confirm all dc.* tables remain byte-identical after formatting or symbolization
When splitting smgp.asm, preserve exact ROM order and use include boundaries that do not change addresses

build_foundation.description: Completed baseline work needed before larger reverse-engineering tasks can proceed safely.

tasks.title: Adopt vermilion-style Windows verification workflow
tasks.description: Replace the old shell-based checksum flow with a Windows-first verify.bat that builds the ROM and checks a known-good SHA256 hash. This gives the project the same one-command bit-perfect verification loop used in vermilion.

tasks.title: Document build and verify workflow for humans and agents
tasks.description: Update README.md and AGENTS.md so build.bat is the direct assembler entry point and verify.bat or varify.bat is the canonical correctness check after any change.

label_coverage.description: Systematically replace loc_ labels with intent-revealing names, using the same label-first discipline that drove vermilion from raw disassembly to readable source.

tasks.title: Rename all JSR- and BSR-called routines in dependency order
tasks.description: Build a call graph from smgp.lst or smgp.asm, then rename every externally called routine before tackling low-value internal branch targets. Start with boot, race loop, rendering, decompression, and menu handlers. This is the highest-leverage readability improvement and should mirror vermilion's early function-renaming campaign.
tasks.progress_notes: All JSR/BSR-called loc_ targets renamed across four batches (0 remaining). Final batch: Decompress_tilemap_to_vdp_64_cell_rows (was loc_7AA, 28 callsites), Load_default_control_type_handler (loc_3C02), Load_control_type_handler (loc_3C06), Draw_car_machine_tilemap (loc_C2CE), Build_minimap_player_row_buffer (loc_C6D6), Build_minimap_standings_row_buffer (loc_C7E0), Advance_road_marker_sequence_b (loc_D164). Also corrected mislabeled Decompress_tilemap_to_vdp_64_cell_rows (was using 128-cell stride) to Decompress_tilemap_to_vdp_128cell_with_tile_offset. 354 named labels total, 3859 loc_ labels remain (mostly branch targets).

tasks.title: Rename high-reference shared branch targets and loop labels
tasks.description: After call targets are named, rename common internal join points, loop backs, returns, and done paths to RoutineName_Loop, RoutineName_Return, RoutineName_Next, and similar local conventions. This makes large routines navigable and is the direct counterpart to vermilion's large batch label cleanup commits.
tasks.progress_notes: COMPLETE. All loc_ label definitions eliminated across 41+ batches. 0 loc_ definitions remain in smgp.asm. Final batch (~1369 new labels): HUD asset/tilemap lists (Race_hud_full_asset_list, Race_hud_tilemap_list, Hud_best/rival/champ_lap_tilemap_list, etc.), Z80 audio engine internals (Audio_engine_seq_*, Audio_engine_ym_*, Audio_engine_psg_*, etc.), sprite frame data cluster ($108D8-$129AE), standings sort buffers, per-track data records for all 19 tracks, championship/endgame/podium assets, result screen data, save/load routines, AI placement data, driver standings display assets. Fixed one incorrect Race_hud_tiles_a->Race_hud_tiles_f substitution in Race_hud_asset_list that broke bit-perfect verification. Build verified bit-perfect SHA256 9046c9d6... Previous: Batches 1-40 complete. Batch 40: Options_screen_cram_data, TeamMessagesBeforeRace, Team_name_strings_table, etc.

tasks.title: Name major data blocks and pointer tables
tasks.description: Rename unnamed dc.l, dc.w, and dc.b tables for tracks, teams, portraits, results, sign assets, graphics blobs, and sound data. Prioritize tables that already have partial notes or are referenced by named routines.
tasks.progress_notes: Batch 26 named all 19 background palette tables, 57 road-style data tables (sideline/road/finish per track), 17 lap-time target tables, 11 sprite-frame pointer tables, 9 crash car frame data labels, and 2 player car sprite frame index tables. Original ROM offset comments preserved for all renamed loc_ labels. Earlier renames should still be audited for consistency project-wide.

tasks.title: Preserve original ROM offsets for every renamed symbol
tasks.description: Ensure every loc_ rename keeps a nearby commented original address such as ;loc_59BC. This safeguards cross-referencing with old notes, emulator traces, and earlier commits while the codebase is still monolithic.
tasks.progress_notes: Naming style is now being standardized for new internal labels using helper-oriented names such as *_Rts, *_Loop, *_Scroll_text, *_Copy_row, and *_special_char_*. This is partially done in championship team-select, save/load, endgame portrait, and text rendering paths but not yet consistent across the whole ROM. Latest audit passes restored explicit ;loc_ ROM-offset preservation comments for renamed team-text data across the $3A6FA-$3C0D8 region and the full team-intro text block at $3B17E-$3B502, including JP/EN intro strings and short helper messages such as Team_intro_jp_1, Team_msg_jp_help_comeback, Team_intro_en_be_top_driver, Team_msg_can_still_drive, Team_msg_envy_high_spirits, Team_msg_hi, Team_msg_jp_after_error, and Team_msg_jp_after_wonderful. More regions still need the same audit.

tasks.title: Standardize local label naming inside major routines
tasks.description: Large subsystems like race update, track parsing, and menu flow should use a consistent local naming style instead of anonymous loc_ chains. Adopt suffixes such as _Loop, _Done, _Return, _Skip, and _Next to match the eventual mature style.
tasks.progress_notes: Now more valuable after normalized source formatting and recent high-impact helper renames; should report 1861 remaining loc_ labels and prioritize internal branch targets by reference count and region.

tasks.title: Name interrupt handlers and state dispatch tables
tasks.description: Identify the vertical blank, horizontal blank, trap, menu-state, race-state, and other dispatch entry points so the top-level control flow becomes legible. These labels should become anchors for later documentation and file splitting.
tasks.progress_notes: Named all 46 Frame_callback and Vblank_callback assignment targets across the full ROM. Labels include all attract, title, pre-race, race result, championship, options, team-select, credits, endgame, and game-over screen init and frame callbacks. Added Default_vblank_handler ($3D8), Default_vblank_handler_h32 ($3E0, h32 variant start), Vblank_tail ($3E6, shared tail), Install_hblank_handler. Renamed loc_32E0->Options_screen_arcade_init, loc_3800->Arcade_race_init, loc_42F8->Race_finish_results_init. Applied 34 symbolic name replacements for backward-reference-safe MOVE.l targets. Corrected a mislabeled Vblank_tail (was named Default_vblank_handler_h32 pointing at wrong offset). All 20 forward-reference MOVE.l targets remain as raw hex literals per the single-pass assembler constraint. Build bit-perfect.

tasks.title: Create automated label coverage reporting
tasks.description: Add a tool that reports total labels, remaining loc_ labels, named call targets, and high-reference unnamed symbols. This gives the project the same measurable progress tracking that vermilion used during its long naming phase.
tasks.progress_notes: Added verified byte-identical helper macros in macros.asm and applied them across smgp.asm. Current macro set includes VDP helpers (vdpComm, VdpRowAdvance, SetH40MenuRegs), interrupt/display helpers (InterruptDisable, InterruptEnable, EnableDisplay, EnableDisplay_Rts, EnableDisplayAfterWait, InterruptWaitEnable_Rts), setup helpers (InitH40ClearObjects, HaltAudioAndEnableAfterWait), and exact whole-block helpers (WaitTwoVblanks, VdpWriteD7D0, DrawPackedTilemapInterrupt_Rts, GetPlayerAndRivalTeamsD0D1, ReturnToTitleMenu, SetPlayerTeamChampionship_Rts, DecompressTilemap64_27x1B). Safe vdpComm replacements now cover multiple repeated command classes including $0000/$0002/$0020/$0E16/$0E24/$1212/$1222/$3E80/$41A0/$63A0/$6B80/$7A00. Build verified bit-perfect.

constants_and_structs.description: Replace raw addresses, offsets, and mode values with symbolic names, then document the key RAM-backed structs that drive gameplay.

tasks.title: Replace remaining raw work RAM references with symbolic constants
tasks.description: Audit all $FFFFxxxx and $00FFxxxx operands in smgp.asm and define symbolic names in constants.asm for any remaining raw RAM accesses. The current constants file covers only a small subset of gameplay state; vermilion reached maturity by making RAM naming nearly universal.
tasks.progress_notes: constants.asm rewritten from 59 lines to ~260 lines with 100+ named constants covering hardware registers, frame dispatch, input, system flags, VDP cache, title/menu state, race event flags, player vehicle state, track/road state, screen scratch variables, tilemap draw queue, pause/music state, lap/overtake flags, race/standings state. 1217+38 raw $FFFFxxxx/$FFFFFFxx literals replaced in smgp.asm. Missing Horizontal_position ($FFFFAE12) and Player_distance ($FFFFAE1A) restored after rewrite. Follow-up RAM audit work added tools/report_raw_ram_refs.py to enumerate remaining raw RAM operands and then continued in multiple naming batches: Screen_state_word_0/1 ($FFFFFC06/$FFFFFC16), Screen_state_byte_0/1/2 ($FFFFFC19/$FFFFFC1A/$FFFFFC1B), Team_select_state ($FFFFFC1E), Player_grid_position_b/Rival_grid_position_b ($FFFFFF35/$FFFFFF3B), font/dialogue/digit buffers, special-road-scene state, road row/scanline buffers, UI tilemap banks, podium minimap state, road-column DMA state, save-buffer aliases, cursor/dialogue scratch bytes, depth-sort/rival-time helpers, and palette-buffer offset expressions replacing many remaining raw $FFFFE98x-$FFFFE9Fx literals while preserving bit-perfect output. The report count has been reduced from 349 unique raw RAM operands at the start of this pass to 290, and each completed batch has been build-verified bit-perfect.

tasks.title: Replace remaining raw hardware, VDP, and Z80 control literals
tasks.description: Define any missing Mega Drive hardware constants for I/O ports, VDP commands, Z80 bus control, and audio handshakes, then replace raw literals throughout the source.
tasks.progress_notes: All raw hardware, VDP, and Z80 control literals replaced. Added: Version_register ($00A10001), Io_ctrl_port_1/2/3_data/dir ($00A10003/$00A10008/$00A1000C etc.), Io_port_settle_l/w, Vdp_plane_row_bytes ($FFFFFF16), Vdp_plane_tile_count ($FFFFFF24), Object_update_counter ($FFFFFF14), Track_preview_index ($FFFFFF28), Boot_init_sentinel ($FFFFFFFC), Sprite_attr_buf ($FFFF9AC0), Road_scale_table ($FFFF9700), Main_object_pool ($FFFFAD80), Aux_object_pool ($FFFFB840), Championship_logo_buf ($FFFFEE80). Replaced all raw $00A11200 (Z80_reset) and $00FF5AC6 (Audio_engine_flags) occurrences. No remaining raw $FFFFxxxx or $00A1xxxx literals in active code. Build verified bit-perfect.

tasks.title: Reverse-engineer the player state struct near $FFFFAE00
tasks.description: The notes mention a probable player state struct around $AE00 with horizontal position, distance, speed, and other runtime values. Identify the full layout, define struct field constants, and replace raw offsets in all related routines.
tasks.progress_notes: Car object struct documented: size $40 bytes, fields at +$00 (fn ptr), +$12 (x pos), +$1A (track dist), +$1E (place score), +$2B (byte), +$30 (max speed), +$32 (max speed*128), +$34 (accel cap). Named Player_obj=$FFFFAE00, Horizontal_position=$FFFFAE12, Player_distance=$FFFFAE1A, Player_place_score=$FFFFAE1E. Defined Car_obj_* offset constants in constants.asm.

tasks.title: Reverse-engineer the AI car struct array near $FFFFB080
tasks.description: Document the full AI car entity layout, define field offsets, and replace raw struct access patterns. This is the racing-game counterpart to vermilion's large object struct cleanup work.
tasks.progress_notes: AI car array at $FFFFB080, 16 entries × $40 bytes. Entry 0 is rival car (Rival_car_obj=$FFFFB080), entries 1-15 are background AI. Defined Ai_car_array, Ai_car_stride, Rival_car_obj, Rival_car_place_score=$FFFFB09E. Same Car_obj_* field offsets apply as for player object. Remaining struct fields (+$04, +$0E, +$16, +$18, +$22, +$26, +$28) not yet named — needs deeper RE.

tasks.title: Define race, menu, and championship state constants
tasks.description: Replace raw mode values controlling practice, warm-up, shift type, race start, result screens, standings, and championship progression with named constants. This will make control flow and conditionals much easier to follow.

tasks.title: Define team, car, engine, and acceleration selector constants
tasks.description: Replace raw values and selectors related to Team_car_characteristics, Engine_data_offset, Acceleration_modifier, points tables, and team-specific car behavior with symbolic constants and per-field comments.
tasks.progress_notes: Added Engine_data_offset_practice = $003C to constants.asm with explanatory comment (practice/warm-up/attract mode uses variant 2 mid-strength engine, not team-specific). Replaced all 3 raw #$003C Engine_data_offset initialization literals in smgp.asm (attract/arcade init at line 3707, free-practice init at line 4786, championship warm-up init at line 7814). Expanded Team_car_acceleration and related constant comments to show byte-offset semantics. Fixed incorrect variant-5 comment (was 'practice default', now just 'weakest'). Updated variant-2 comment to note Engine_data_offset_practice. Build verified bit-perfect.

tasks.title: Reorganize constants.asm with sections and access-size notes
tasks.description: Add clear hardware, input, RAM map, struct field, and mode sections to constants.asm. Include byte or word access expectations where non-obvious, mirroring the way vermilion eventually turned constants into a primary source of documentation.
tasks.progress_notes: Completed the constants.asm reorganization pass. The file now uses explicit section blocks for hardware, input, RAM state, object structs, audio, mode values, and work buffers, and this batch filled in remaining access-size/purpose notes for core unannotated symbols such as Input_state_bitset, Input_click_bitset, Player_team, Rival_team, Track_index_arcade_mode, Race_started, Practice_mode, Warm_up, Player_shift, Player_rpm, Player_speed, Visual_rpm, Steering_output, and Road_x_offset. constants.asm now reads as the primary symbolic RAM map reference.

documentation_core.description: Raise documentation quality from isolated comments to subsystem-level explanations, with the same emphasis on function headers and control-flow commentary that made vermilion readable.

tasks.title: Document vectors, ROM header, startup checks, and hardware init
tasks.description: Add routine headers and explanatory comments for StartOfRom, the exception vector table, ROM header fields, checksum handling, and EntryPoint startup logic. This should explain the boot sequence clearly enough that a new contributor can follow the first few hundred lines without external notes.
tasks.progress_notes: Added full block comment before StartOfRom explaining vector table layout with all 64 vector offsets and active handler summary. ROM header fully documented with offset/size/description table for all 16 fields. ErrorTrap1/2/3 explained with per-class description. EntryPoint documented with a 6-step boot sequence overview covering: TMSS check/warm-reboot shortcut, cold-boot VDP/Z80/RAM pre-init, ROM checksum verification, first-boot lap time init + language detection, full work RAM + hardware init, and main frame loop. All EntryPoint instructions now have inline comments. loc_4B4 register init table annotated. loc_510A renamed Initialize_default_lap_times with header comment. loc_6428 renamed Initialize_ui_tilemap_buffers with header comment. Wait_for_vblank comment corrected (no longer a question). loc_57C documented as LCG PRNG with algorithm description. Build verified bit-perfect.

tasks.title: Document the main per-frame race loop and update order
tasks.description: Identify the core frame loop for gameplay and document the order of input sampling, RPM updates, shift logic, speed integration, AI updates, VDP writes, and race-state transitions. This is the most important missing high-level explanation in the current codebase.
tasks.progress_notes: Added full Race_loop header comment documenting all 25 steps in per-frame update order. Race_loop is at $36B6. Replaced raw #$000036B6 forward references with #Race_loop in Championship_race_init and Arcade_race_init. Build bit-perfect.

tasks.title: Document the driving model subsystems
tasks.description: Complete the documentation around Update_shift, Update_rpm, Update_visual_rpm, speed, braking, steering, crash, and retire behavior. Include how physical constants are interpreted and where decimal literals reflect game design rather than raw encoding.
tasks.progress_notes: Added function header comments to all 8 driving model routines: Update_shift (gear ratio math, auto-shift thresholds), Update_rpm (Acceleration_data indexing, sub-routine call chain, pre-race rev animation, crash decel), Update_visual_rpm (±80/150 per-frame interpolation), Update_engine_sound_pitch ($022E/$0E86 shift-warning pulse), Update_speed (RPM→km/h via Engine_data table, ±2/5 rate limit), Update_breaking (braking strength table, track-quality modifier), Update_steering (Road_x_offset zones, boundary wobble, auto-centre, dead zone), Update_horizontal_position (curve displacement, steering integration, collision flag). Added 14 new symbolic constants to constants.asm: Control_key_shift_down/up/accel/brake, Crash_animation_flag, Crash_spin_flag, Retire_animation_flag, Spin_off_track_flag, Overtake_position_delta, Track_steering_index/b, Track_braking_index, Replay_steer_override, Engine_sound_pitch. Replaced 34 additional raw literals in smgp.asm. Build verified bit-perfect.

tasks.title: Document rendering, HUD, and road display pipeline
tasks.description: Explain the speedometer, RPM gauge, minimap, sign rendering, road displacement, background rotation, and VDP command flow. This should include the interaction between decompressed track data and on-screen presentation.
tasks.progress_notes: Second batch complete: added/improved function header comments for all remaining rendering/HUD/road-display routines. Covered: Write_tilemap_rows_to_vdp (VDP row writer with tile base offset), Draw_packed_tilemap_to_vdp + Draw_packed_tilemap_to_vdp_preset_base (RLE packed stream decoder), Decompress_to_vdp (Huffman tile decompressor → VDP), Decompress_to_ram (same → RAM), Build_decompression_code_table (flat decode table builder), Build_car_tilemap_buffers (6 forward car frames + 4 priority-flip mirror buffers), Initialize_car_tile_scroll (renamed from loc_1A7C; car DMA initializer), Update_background_scroll_delta (per-frame road-car scroll ring update), Update_slope_data (40-entry slope integrator + scale + interpolate pipeline), Integrate_curveslope (expanded header: streak integrator, direction flag, carry sentinel), Render_text_to_tilemap (expanded: control byte decode table, JP/EN buffer layout), Draw_bcd_time_to_vdp (BCD time formatter → VDP), Compute_minimap_index + Load_minimap_position (track-position → minimap (x,y)), Build_minimap_player_row_buffer (renamed from loc_C6D6), Build_minimap_standings_row_buffer (renamed from loc_C7E0), Advance_road_marker_sequence_b (renamed from loc_D164). All 4 callsites for renamed labels updated. Build verified bit-perfect.

tasks.title: Document race flow, menus, and championship progression
tasks.description: Add headers and comments covering attract mode, title screen, team selection, practice and warm-up modes, race start countdown, results, standings, and the multi-race championship progression.
tasks.progress_notes: Added a GAME FLOW OVERVIEW block comment before the first screen routine documenting the full Frame_callback dispatch chain: attract cycle, world championship path (16 races), arcade path, and free-practice path. Added function header comments to all 28 screen init and frame routines: Race_preview_screen_init, Attract_screen_logo_frame, Title_menu, Title_anim_frame, Title_menu_frame, Championship_start_init, Options_screen_arcade_init, Options_screen_init, Options_setup_frame, Championship_race_init, Arcade_race_init, Race_loop (pre-existing), Championship_warmup_race_frame, Practice_mode_init, Race_results_frame, Race_finish_results_init, Pre_race_display_frame, Pre_race_preview_car_frame, Pre_race_screen_championship_init, Race_result_overlay_frame, Championship_next_race_init, Driver_standings_frame, Driver_standings_init, Team_select_frame, Team_select_screen_init, Championship_standings_init, Championship_team_select_frame, Championship_podium_frame, Championship_standings_2_init. Build verified bit-perfect.

tasks.title: Document AI car behavior and placement logic
tasks.description: Identify the routines and data tables that govern AI speed, position, lane movement, collisions, placement, and points. Add comments that tie RAM structures to race behavior and scoreboard updates.

tasks.title: Annotate major data tables entry-by-entry
tasks.description: Tables for teams, drivers, portraits, track assets, messages, points, and signs should have per-entry labels or comments so data can be edited safely without constantly referring to the ROM in a debugger.

data_formats_and_assets.description: Decode the remaining opaque gameplay and asset formats so track, sign, art, and text data are understandable enough to support future editing tools.

tasks.title: Fully decode the Track_data record layout
tasks.description: Document every field in Track_data, including pointers, lengths, tilesets, background assets, minimap assets, decompression targets, and any track-specific behavior flags. Every track entry should become self-describing.
tasks.progress_notes: All 19 Track_data entries (16 championship + 3 arcade Monaco variants) fully documented. Each entry is $48 bytes. Fields: +$00..+$1C 8×pointer (minimap tiles, BG tiles, BG mapping, minimap mapping, palette, sideline style, road style, finish line style), +$20 horizon override flag, +$22 track length, +$24 signs data ptr, +$28 signs tileset ptr, +$2C minimap position map ptr, +$30 curve data ptr, +$34 visual slope data ptr, +$38 physical slope data ptr, +$3C lap time base ptr, +$40 lap target time table ptr, +$44 steering sensitivity divisors (high=straight, low=curve). Added constants: Track_horizon_override_flag, Track_phys_slope_value, Visual_slope_data, Physical_slope_data, Steering_divisor_straight/curve, Track_lap_time_records, Track_lap_target_buf, Track_lap_time_base_ptr. All raw literals replaced with symbolic names throughout smgp.asm. Full structured comment block before Track_data and Load_track_data. Build verified bit-perfect.

tasks.title: Crack slope data encoding and parser behavior
tasks.description: Finish the open notes.txt task for slope data. Determine the exact ROM encoding, decompression behavior, integrator math, sentinel semantics, and any edge cases that currently freeze or glitch. Replace the partial notes with precise source-level documentation.
tasks.progress_notes: ROM encoding fully documented in smgp.asm (Update_slope_data, Integrate_curveslope headers). Visual slope uses 3/4-byte RLE records with initial background-displacement byte and per-record delta. Physical slope uses simpler 3-byte RLE. Both streams terminate with high-byte bit 7 set (BMI). Decompressed byte encoding identical to curve data: $00=flat, $01-$2F=downslope, $41-$6F=upslope, $80+=$FF sentinel/wrap. Track_phys_slope_value drives RPM drag in Update_rpm. notes.txt updated to SOLVED.

audio_and_z80.description: Bring the audio side of the ROM up to the same documentation standard as the 68K game logic, eventually ending with a real Z80 disassembly rather than an opaque data blob.

tasks.title: Crack sign data format and per-track signage flow
tasks.description: Finish the open notes.txt task for signs. Identify the sign record layout, tileset switching rules, parser state machine, pointer usage, placement semantics, and any compressed or banked data involved.
tasks.progress_notes: Sign data stream format fully documented: 4-byte records (distance.w, count.b, sign_id.b), $FFFF sentinel resets to stream start each lap. Tileset stream: 4-byte records (distance.w, tileset_offset.w), $FFFF sentinel. Sign_lookup_table: .l pointers to $FF-terminated frame-index lists. Values 1-$14=normal signs (aux object handler from loc_8B1C), $15+=special objects (tunnel etc., handler to $FFFFAFC0). Signs_in_row_count signs spaced $0010 apart at Signs_location. Finish-line flagkeeper spawned when player within 120 units of track end (Finish_line_sign_active flag). Added 10 sign RAM constants to constants.asm ($FFFF9240-$FFFF925C). All raw literals replaced in Parse_tileset_for_signs and Parse_sign_data with symbolic names and full header comments added. notes.txt updated to SOLVED.

architecture_and_extraction.description: Refactor the monolithic source into a maintainable include-based layout while preserving ROM order and exact bytes, following the same structural maturation that vermilion went through.

tasks.title: Split smgp.asm into an include-based top-level file
tasks.description: Convert smgp.asm from a 43K-line monolith into a thin top-level file that includes ordered modules for header, init, gameplay, data, graphics, and audio. Preserve original ROM layout exactly. This is the single biggest architectural step needed to reach vermilion-like maintainability.

tasks.title: Extract header, vectors, and startup code into dedicated modules
tasks.description: Move StartOfRom, Vectors, Header, ErrorTrap handlers, and EntryPoint-related initialization into their own include files. These top-of-ROM structures are natural, low-risk first splits.

tasks.title: Split gameplay and rendering subsystems into thematic modules
tasks.description: Create separate include files for input, driving model, race flow, rendering, menus, AI, and track parsing once the relevant labels and docs exist. This is the direct analogue of vermilion's code-region split into src/*.asm modules.

tasks.title: Split major data tables and asset regions into dedicated modules
tasks.description: Move track data, team and driver tables, text data, graphics data, and sound/Z80 data into ordered include files. This makes later tooling and extraction work dramatically safer.

tasks.title: Extract large binary blobs with incbin where safe
tasks.description: Once assets are clearly identified, move suitable large binary data blocks to external files referenced by incbin, using helper labels and comments to preserve exact offsets. Do this only for stable, well-understood data regions.

macro_refactors.description: Introduce macros and record helpers only after the underlying patterns are understood well enough to prove byte-identical expansion.

tasks.title: Add VDP command and row-advance helper macros
tasks.description: Identify repeated VDP control longwords and address-step patterns in rendering code, then wrap them in macros that assemble identically. This should follow the same careful macro discipline vermilion used for DMA and VDP helpers.

tasks.title: Add Z80 bus control helper macros
tasks.description: After AUDIO-002 documents the handshake, create small macros for request, release, and wait patterns around Z80 access to reduce duplicated low-level sequences without changing bytes.

tasks.title: Create record macros for track and team data
tasks.description: Once Track_data and team-related tables are fully decoded, define readable macros for their entry formats so data edits become less error-prone and easier to review.

tasks.title: Annotate duplicated patterns that must not yet be deduplicated
tasks.description: Some repeated instruction or data patterns may look macro-worthy but encode subtle layout or branch-range constraints. Document these cases explicitly so future cleanup efforts do not accidentally break the match.

tooling_and_quality.description: Build the exploration, lint, extraction, and visualization tools that a mature disassembly needs for sustained progress and safe editing.

tasks.title: Create label coverage and call graph reporter
tasks.description: Write a Python tool that parses smgp.lst or smgp.asm and reports named call targets, remaining loc_ labels, high-reference unnamed labels, and suggested rename batches. This should become the backbone for LABEL-001 progress tracking.

tasks.title: Create a safe exact-block matcher for ASM macro and rename work
tasks.description: Build a token-aware helper that can locate exact instruction blocks in smgp.asm even when formatting changes, then emit candidate replacements and verification batches. This would reduce the risk of raw regex edits and make future macro extraction and label cleanup more systematic.

tasks.title: Name horizontal blank handler stub and frame callback pointers
tasks.description: Follow LABEL-006 by naming the copied hblank stub at $FFFFFFD2 and the RAM callback pointers used via $FFFFFF10 and $FFFFFF0C, so top-level scheduling becomes more self-describing.
tasks.progress_notes: Added Hblank_handler_stub = $FFFFFFD2 to constants.asm. Renamed loc_406 -> Hblank_handler_stub_src with ;loc_406 comment preserved. Replaced dc.l $FFFFFFD2 in vector table with Hblank_handler_stub. Replaced LEA $FFFFFFD2.w in Install_hblank_handler with Hblank_handler_stub.w. Replaced $FFFFFF10.w and $FFFFFF0C.w in main loop dispatch, VBI dispatch, and startup initialization with Frame_callback.w and Vblank_callback.w. Build verified bit-perfect.

tasks.title: Create a JSON updater for todos progress metadata
tasks.description: Automate updates to roadmap metadata such as remaining loc_ count, label progress text, and completed macro batches so todos.json stays aligned with actual reverse-engineering progress.

modding_foundation.description: Use the mature disassembly and extraction work to support real editor-style tooling tailored to a racing game instead of an RPG randomizer stack.

tasks.title: Write a modding architecture document
tasks.description: Define how extracted JSON or structured data maps back to assembly, which assets are safe to edit automatically, where checksum patching fits, and how verify.bat remains the ultimate correctness gate.

tasks.title: Build a track editor CLI
tasks.description: Create a tool that can edit structured track records, slope data, sign placement, and associated asset references, then write the results back to assembly safely. This is the most valuable modding tool unique to Super Monaco GP.

tasks.title: Build a team, driver, and car stats editor CLI
tasks.description: Create an editor for team order, driver mappings, portraits, car characteristics, engine tables, acceleration tables, and points-related data. This should support common gameplay hacks without direct assembly editing.

tasks.title: Build a text and sign message editor CLI
tasks.description: Create an editor for text tables, team messages, sign text assets if any, and language-dependent strings, validating encoding against the txt macro and any format-specific constraints.

tasks.title: Build a championship and progression data editor CLI
tasks.description: Create an editor for standings, points awards, race order, practice and warm-up flags, and other championship progression data once those structures are fully named and documented.

regression_and_notes.description: Prevent regressions and turn scattered notes into a maintained project knowledge base instead of a scratchpad.

tasks.title: Create a run_checks aggregator script
tasks.description: Add a script that runs verify.bat plus the project's coverage, comment-density, and raw-literal linters in one place. This becomes the standard pre-commit quality gate once tooling exists.

tasks.title: Add pre-commit hook scripts for Windows and Bash
tasks.description: Provide simple hook installers that run the check aggregator and verify.bat automatically. This mirrors vermilion's later regression-prevention work and makes bit-perfect discipline easier to maintain.

tasks.title: Add targeted lints for raw loc_ labels and raw RAM references
tasks.description: After label and constant coverage improves, add lints that flag newly introduced raw loc_ references in cleaned regions and any reintroduced raw RAM or I/O literals that should use constants instead.

tasks.title: Modernize notes.txt into a maintained reverse-engineering index
tasks.description: Update notes.txt so solved items point to canonical labels and comments in source, RAM locations use symbolic names, and remaining unknowns are clearly separated from already-understood subsystems.
tasks.progress_notes: Complete rewrite of notes.txt. Removed stale TODO entries for solved topics (slope data, signs data). Split into OPEN PROBLEMS (artwork ID, Z80 disasm, music IDs, AI behavior, $A980 struct) and SOLVED PROBLEMS (slope, signs, track data, curve data, player struct, AI array, label coverage) sections. LOCATIONS section fully replaced with symbolic constant names from constants.asm. Team intro sprite labels now use Intro_sprite_data_* names matching smgp.asm. Curve data and slope data decompressed format documentation preserved with updated symbolic references. Build verified bit-perfect.

tasks.title: Maintain an explicit open-problems index for unresolved subsystems
tasks.description: Keep a short list of still-unsolved topics such as slope edge cases, signs edge cases, artwork identification, Z80 audio gaps, and music IDs, with pointers to the relevant routines and tools. This prevents TODOs from disappearing into commit history.
tasks.progress_notes: Addressed as part of NOTES-001 rewrite. notes.txt now has a clear OPEN PROBLEMS section listing: artwork/tileset identification, Z80 driver disassembly, music ID enumeration, AI behavior, and $A980 struct. Each points to the relevant area of smgp.asm or constants.asm.

priority_order: --- Phase 1A: Core readability and control flow ---
